detail: GWAS Catalog association with p=7e-18. Effect: OR/Beta=0.044723 [0.035-0.055] SD units decrease. Initial sample: 89,266 East Asian ancestry individuals. Replication sample: NA.
whyItMatters: This common-variant association is matched locally against the uploaded genotype and should be treated as a tendency signal, not a prediction.
riskSummary: CDKN2B-AS1 risk allele is associated with Lymphocyte count (OR/Beta 0.044723 [0.035-0.055] SD units decrease) in a large, replicated genome-wide study
notes: Generated from bulk GWAS Catalog data; effect size and ancestry transferability require review.

title: possible miscall in Ancestry v2.0 datasets; otherwise, probably more susceptible to influenza
summary: possible miscall in Ancestry v2.0 datasets; otherwise, probably more susceptible to influenza
detail: rs12252(C;C) individuals have been reported in one study to be more susceptible to influenza and to complications from it. This has also been flagged by Promethease (as of June 2016) as potentially a SNP prone to being miscalled in data derived from the AncestryDNA v2.0 chips.
whyItMatters: SNPedia has a genotype-specific page for this result, which can add consumer-facing context alongside primary evidence sources.

title: carrier of one allele linked to greater influenza susceptibility
summary: carrier of one allele linked to greater influenza susceptibility
detail: Carrying one copy of the rs12252(C) allele does not seem to make a person more susceptible to influenza, however, carrying two copies does, so this (C;T) genotype is tagged as a (unaffected) carrier of an allele linked to influenza susceptibility.
